    57	              <HeadingAnchorPlugin />

    11	// Dev-only: resolve @shiro/*/style.css to source or empty virtual module.

    14	function workspaceCssPlugin(): Plugin {

    10	type ImageLoadState = 'loading' | 'loaded' | 'error'

    12	function getCaptionText(altText: string, caption?: string): string | undefined {
    13	  if (caption) return caption
    14	  if (!altText) return undefined
    15	  if (altText.startsWith('!') || altText.startsWith('¡')) {
    16	    return altText.slice(1)
    17	  }
    18	  return undefined
    19	}
    20

    45

    51	            className={`rr-image-frame rr-image-${state}`}
    52	            style={frameStyle}
    53	            role="button"

    56	            onKeyDown={(e) => {

    58	              e.preventDefault()
    59	              e.currentTarget.click()
    60	            }}

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex 2026-02-15 15:02:23Z)**_

**Findings**
1. [High] Demo dev wiring is incomplete for the new mention package, so references/styles can break in `vite serve`.
   - `@shiro/rich-renderers` now pulls mention via `packages/rich-renderers/src/config.ts:7` and `packages/rich-renderers/src/style.css:6`.
   - But `packages/rich-editor-demo/vite.config.ts:15` does not include `rich-renderer-mention` in `plainCssSrc`, so `@shiro/rich-renderer-mention/style.css` is treated as empty by `packages/rich-editor-demo/vite.config.ts:37`.
   - Also no source alias exists for `@shiro/rich-renderer-mention` alongside other renderer aliases in `packages/rich-editor-demo/vite.config.ts:91`.

2. [Medium] `enhancedRendererConfig` does not use the new edit-capable mention renderer.
   - `packages/rich-renderers/src/config.ts:16` maps `Mention` to `MentionRenderer`.
   - `packages/rich-renderer-mention/src/MentionEditRenderer.tsx:13` already gates by mode and falls back to display renderer outside editor, so using it in config would enable editing in editor mode without renderer-mode downside.

3. [Medium] Core fallback mention is now unstyled after mention-style removal.
   - Fallback still emits mention classes in `packages/rich-editor/src/components/renderers/MentionRenderer.tsx:15`.
   - Mention styling rules were removed from `packages/rich-editor/src/styles/shared.css.ts` and `packages/rich-editor/src/styles/note.css.ts`.
   - Result: core-only usage (without `@shiro/rich-renderer-mention`) now renders mentions as plain text.

4. [Low] Fallback output is inconsistent between core and package for plain mentions.
   - Core fallback prefixes `@` in `packages/rich-editor/src/components/renderers/MentionRenderer.tsx:16`.
   - Package fallback (unknown platform path) does not in `packages/rich-renderer-mention/src/MentionRenderer.tsx:118`.
   - Same content can render differently depending on whether override config is used.

**Checks Run**
1. `pnpm -C packages/rich-renderer-mention exec tsc --noEmit` passed.
2. `pnpm -C packages/rich-renderers exec tsc --noEmit` passed.
